interpretation: OVERLOAD: peak consumption exceeds entire utility capacity. Extreme peaking factor is the binding constraint.
SEVERE: peak consumption claims 60% of utility capacity. Large facility footprint amplified by high PF.
SEVERE: 57% of utility consumed. Rural Oklahoma utility; demand scale dominates.
HIGH: 49% of utility consumed. Small-city utility with a large facility on demand-scale basis.
HIGH: 31% consumed. Large county utility; arid climate drives high r.
HIGH: extreme PF = 30 drives 27% peak load despite very small average withdrawal.
MODERATE: 14%. Permitted 2 MGD against 28 MGD utility.
LOW: 1.3%. Massive 258 MGD Memphis utility absorbs the facility load.
MINIMAL: 3.5%. Midlothian's 36 MGD system comfortably absorbs facility demand, though DC share of community use is notable given the small population.
MINIMAL: 0.2%. SNWA's 900 MGD system makes facility contribution negligible.
